other_observers: Tim Henson, Gary Matthews, Bill Kaempfer, Bruce Bosley and all other Sterling CBC participants. Also Connie and Rick Steincamp and Bill Schmoker. Bill Kaempfer re-found the owl in the afternoon.; Tim Henson and I found this bird while doing the Sterling CBC. Tim has indicated that he has no interest in submitting the CBRC information, so I agreed to do this using one of his pictures.
specific_location: One half mile west of Logan CR 33 on Logan CR 44. It was about 200 yards north of CR 44.
description: A mostly white fairly large owl sitting on the ground.   The round flat face was all bright white; eyes yellow; the chest and back were bright white and barred or patterned heavily with brown; the top of the head reminded this viewer of a bad combover of brown fuzz.Behaviors: It sat on the ground in a field of corn or similar plant stubble. It looked around swiveling the head almost 360 degrees total. It otherwise did not move.Call: N/APlumage: immature
